prompt:
Use the image just shown as the visual style reference only. Match its 16-bit pixel art style closely: crisp chunky pixels, 1px dark outline color #141019, flat 2-3 step shading, warm arcade mining palette, clean readable silhouette, same pixel density and sprite scale feel. Do NOT draw or copy the miner.

Create a raw sprite animation sheet for a new side-view cave-mutant enemy named Smoocher. Exact sheet: 2 rows by 3 columns, exactly 6 equal invisible cells, read left-to-right across the top row then left-to-right across the bottom row. Background must be 100% solid flat #FF00FF magenta only, no transparency, no gradients, no scenery. No visible grid, no borders, no labels, no text, no UI.

Subject identity in every cell: cute goofy wholesome round waddling blob creature, gob-green body #8cd94a, stubby little feet, tiny arms held close, big cartoon eyes, oversized pursed cartoon lips as the defining feature. Family-friendly and silly, not gross, not scary. Side-view facing right. Dark #141019 outline, dark cavern-brown underside accents, small warm lantern-gold highlights, flat 2-3 shade bands, crisp 16-bit pixel art.

Action: 6-frame side-view walk/waddle loop. The same body size in all frames, stable bottom/feet anchor line across every cell, body centered in the central 60-70% safe area of each cell. Motion phases: neutral rounded stance; squash down as front foot plants and lips lead forward; stretch up slightly with tiny rear foot stepping; rounded mid-waddle; squash on the other foot with lips puckered forward; return stretch into loop. Lips lead the way but stay attached to the face and do not widen the bounding box too much.

Containment: full body must fit inside each cell, leave generous magenta margin on all four sides, same silhouette scale and same character identity every frame, no body part or lips crossing a cell edge, no floating detached effects, no shadows outside the sprite.
